behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily a module-handoff/serialization contract (classifier -> request builder -> API client). A unit test of only one helper can miss dropped labels at wiring boundaries (MOCK-MISS), but real OS/network behavior is not required and no model-output judgment is involved. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified because the behavior text does not require validating real external response/error contracts or round-trip side effects; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because correctness does not depend on model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run the classifier with real request-construction and client-adapter modules, but a stub transport that captures outbound payload; assert every taxonomy label appears in the API request for multiple taxonomies.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure risk is at boundary interaction between response parsing and taxonomy validation, not just a single pure function. Integration prevents false confidence from unit-only parsing tests by exercising module wiring. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified because no real external contract/round-trip effect is required in the behavior text; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because this asserts deterministic guardrail logic over a provided label, not model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use real parser/normalizer/validator pipeline with a stubbed API response returning out-of-taxonomy labels; assert final module output is always "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is a deterministic normalization property of one local logic boundary with no I/O and no cross-module reality signal needed. Unit tests are sufficient; higher-level tests add little new evidence for this specific invariant.
behaviors.test_description: Property-style unit test for normalization: feed identical raw response payloads repeatedly and assert identical normalized label output every run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on repeated live AI-provider outputs and consistency of model decisions. A stub cannot reveal real-model variability (LLM-DEP). Workflow rejected because this is a single model-decision loop, not multiple dependent agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Execute repeated real provider calls for fixed input/taxonomy under controlled settings; record returned labels and provider raw outputs; assert label stability when provider response content is unchanged, and allow label changes only when raw provider response differs.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration between tokenizer/budget policy/request dispatcher modules; unit-only tests of token math can miss pre-dispatch enforcement at wiring points. Real external infra is not required by the text, and model-output quality is not involved.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real token-counting, policy, and dispatch-guard modules with a fake API transport; test over-budget documents and assert policy action (truncate or reject) occurs before any outbound dispatch.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires a real external happy-path contract check on provider-reported usage fields (SYSTEM TRIAD #1). Mocked usage can hide provider-side accounting differences (MOCK-HIDE). Agentic was considered, but disqualified because the behavior text targets provider usage contract/compliance rather than model-decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Send representative real requests to the provider and assert returned usage metadata (prompt/total tokens as exposed by provider API) is <= configured per-call budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is retry orchestration across modules (error mapping, backoff, retry loop, final return path). This can be validated with controlled stubbed provider responses without requiring real OS/network effects. System rejected because behavior text does not explicitly require validating real provider error-shape contract; Agentic/Workflow rejected because no model-output quality or multi-agent chain is required.
behaviors.test_description: Use real retry/backoff logic with a stub provider that emits rate-limit responses then success; assert the module waits (virtual/fake clock), retries expected times, and returns successful label instead of surfacing immediate error.
